Semantic lexicons, DSLs, and formal ontologies solve this.

They give LLMs a shared vocabulary with unambiguous referents; typed, scoped, and grounded in meaning.

OWL. RDF. SKOS. SPARQL. These aren’t just data formats.
They’re the grammar of machine cognition.
Minimum structure. Maximum meaning.

Natural language is rich in implicature, metaphor, and cultural context; beautiful for humans, catastrophic for precision.

Every under-specified token is a probability cloud the model must collapse at inference time.

Ambiguity isn’t poetry. In an LLM pipeline, it’s latency and drift.

#AI

You’ve been prompting LLMs in the wrong language this whole time.

Human language is an ambiguous, lossy protocol.

Semantic lexicons exist for a reason.

It’s time to stop whispering to machines in prose. 🧵

A static blue-to-orange gradient at the top of your screen feels dead after five minutes. But a sky that actually shifts through dawn blues, golden hours, twilight purples, and star-filled blacks? That's the difference between a wallpaper you tolerate and one you actually admire. Building an android day night sky gradient system is the first step toward making your live wallpaper respond to the real world outside the phone.

external.description: Apple's recently launched MacBook Neo, positioned as the company's most affordable laptop starting at $599, has received praise from repair experts at iFixit for significant improvements in repairability. In their detailed teardown, iFixit highlights the MacBook Neo as the most repairable MacBook in approximately 14 years. Key positive changes include a battery secured in a tray with 18 screws instead of strong adhesives common in prior models, making battery replacement much simpler and earning cheers from the iFixit team. Additional repair-friendly features encompass a straightforward, flat disassembly process, compatibility of replacement parts with Apple's Repair Assistant without issues, and easier access for swapping the display and keyboard. Despite these advances, iFixit notes ongoing limitations typical of Apple devices, such as soldered RAM and storage that prevent user upgrades. These factors contributed to a final repairability score of 6 out of 10—a solid rating for a MacBook, though not perfect. The MacBook Neo, powered by the A18 Pro chip and featuring a 13-inch Liquid Retina display, aims to combine accessibility, performance, and better longevity through improved serviceability, aligning with growing right-to-repair demands.
external.title: iFixit Teardown Gives Apple's New MacBook Neo a 6/10 Repairability Score, Best in 14 Years

record.text:
University of Maryland Develops Wearable Device to Measure Human Flatulence and Gut Activity

🤖 IA: It's clickbait ⚠️
👥 Usuarios: It's clickbait ⚠️

#gutmicrobiome #flatulence #wearabletechnology 

View full AI summary:

external.description: Researchers at the University of Maryland have introduced a wearable device called Smart Underwear, designed to objectively monitor human flatulence by detecting hydrogen gas produced by gut microbes. Traditional methods of measuring intestinal gas relied on small sample sizes, self-reporting, or invasive techniques, which often underestimated the frequency of flatus. Initial trials with the Smart Underwear revealed that participants passed gas an average of 32 times per day, significantly higher than the 14 times previously cited in medical literature. The device also allows continuous monitoring of gut microbial activity, offering a way to track dietary or probiotic effects on fermentation in real life. To further understand normal and abnormal flatus patterns, the researchers are launching the Human Flatus Atlas, a nationwide study collecting data from hundreds of participants, including stool samples to analyze gut microbiomes. Special attention will be given to different types of gas producers, such as 'Zen Digesters,' who produce minimal gas despite high-fiber diets, and 'Hydrogen Hyperproducers,' who experience frequent gas. This research could establish baseline measures for healthy gut microbial activity and inform future interventions for digestive health.
external.title: University of Maryland Develops Wearable Device to Measure Human Flatulence and Gut Activity
